medical_history.history_of_present_illness: This patient presented with a large goiter with compressive symptoms and primary hyperparathyroidism. Preoperative localization with sestamibi ultrasound and 4-D CAT scan was vague at best, but there was a suggestion of an enlarged parathyroid in the left lower position. This patient had a lupus coagulant factor and was therefore kept on Coumadin for the entire operation with an INR of 2 that was confirmed just before surgery. Preop PTH was 97 in the holding area.
medical_history.past_medical_history: 's thyroiditis, Primary hyperparathyroidism, Lupus anticoagulant with DVT and PE. Surgical history: IVC filter removal, Hysterectomy.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS: 97.8 78 117/58 18 98%RA. GEN: A&O, NAD. HEENT: No scleral icterus, mucus membranes moist, incision site c/d/i, no surrounding erythema or swelling. CV: RRR, No M/G/R. PULM: Clear to auscultation b/l, No W/R/R. ABD: soft, non-distended, nontender. Ext: No edema, warm and well perfused.
